title: Venlyss Pnorr

{{Quote|I've seen a lot in my time, and I don't consider myself fainthearted, but even I found them to be a bit…unsettling. However they are effective|Airen Cracken on Findsman rituals|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Venlyss Pnorr''' was a well-known [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] [[Findsman/Legends|findsman]], a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] religious tradition of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He secretly worked for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] where he used his talents to retrieve endangered Rebels or capture [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] targets.

==Biography==
Venlyss Pnorr secretly worked for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] under the guise of a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. He "captured" endangered Alliance [[operative]]s and returned them to safehouses. He also hunted down [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] targets to deliver to the Alliance however many Imperial captives suffered "accidents" that resulted in their termination prior to being turned over to the Rebels. Pnorr refused to elaborate on the cause behind these events and the Alliance chose to overlook the occasional dead body and suspicious circumstances.

His most recent mission for the Alliance was to rescue an undercover agent from the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] sector headquarters. The agent was "accidentally" killed long after pursuit had been abandoned. The body was delivered to the drop-off point and Pnorr then went into hiding. [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] has ordered him to be brought in for questioning but was unsuccessful believing the [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] was using his [[Findsman/Legends|Findsman]] rituals to reveal who was hunting him and thus was able to stay one step ahead of pursuit.<ref name="CRO" />

==Personality and traits==
Although he worked for the Alliance, Pnorr seemed to have a hidden agenda as he often killed the Imperials he was supposed to be capturing alive. When his actions resulted in the death of an Alliance agent he wisely went into hiding.<ref name="CRO" />

==Skills and abilities==
Competent at both tracking and combat, Pnorr was a solid bounty hunter. He was not the most skilled tracker in the galaxy but his rituals allowed him to get a huge advantage in finding his targets.<ref name="CRO" />

==Equipment==
Pnorr carried protective equipment, a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]], a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and a [[Breath mask/Legends|breathing mask]].<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Venlyss Pnorr was designed by [[Stephen Crane]] for ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1994]]. He was illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]].
